aboutText: A cybersecurity enthusiast focused on ethical hacking, digital forensics, and system hardening. Experienced in vulnerability research, automation, and secure development, committed to building safer tech through innovation and teamwork.

education.degree: Bachelor's & Master's in Computer Science & Engineering with Cybersecurity [Integrated]

strengths.title: Risk Assessment & Management
strengths.description: Ability to assess risks and take decisions accordingly.

strengths.title: Communication
strengths.description: Effective in communicating with team members and in general technical discussions.

strengths.title: Leadership
strengths.description: Demonstrates leadership in team environments and possesses effective communication skills.

strengths.title: Teamwork
strengths.description: Ability to work well in teams to achieve common goals and project success.

certifications.title: ISEA CYBERSECURITY AWARNESS COMPETITION 2025 by MeitY
certifications.description: Secured AIR 1 in Technical Paper submission related to Cybersecurity Domain.

certifications.title: CYBERSECURITY TRAINING FOR CENTRAL DEPARTMENT
certifications.description: Successfully completed the Quiz organised by MeIty.

certifications.title: G20's STAY SAFE CAMPAIGN
certifications.description: Participated for the Cyber hygene Practices,organised by CDAC,Hyderabad under G20's Presidency.

certifications.title: Amazon Academy - Introduction to Cloud
certifications.description: Learned Cloud Infrastructure & Basics via AWS academy!

certifications.title: Industry 4.0 Best Practice & Cybersecurity Landscape
certifications.description: Workshop on Cybersecurity perspective of Industry 4.0 & its best practice

projects.title: Efficient Image Upscaling for Low-End Graphics Cards
projects.description: Contributed to research on hybrid, lightweight upscaling techniques combining spatial enhancement and adaptive neural networks. The goal was to deliver high-quality images while minimizing GPU workload and VRAM usage, making near-native resolution viable for budget hardware.

projects.title: Kali-JS: Live Kali Linux in Browser
projects.description: A live Kali Linux instance booted inside any modern browser using WebAssembly and v86. No VM or ISO required. All changes persist in browser's local storage, offering a seamless, sandboxed environment for security testing and education.

projects.title: Troubleshoot.ai
projects.description: An AI assistant that provides visual, step-by-step resolutions for IT issues. Built using OpenRouter API and web technologies to assist with network, OS, and browser-related problems.

projects.title: UEFI Disk Cloning Utility™
projects.description: Clones entire disks or partitions directly from UEFI without needing an OS. Designed for DFIR professionals. Supports GPT, resume-after-failure, hashing, NVRAM logging, and backup GPT restoration.

projects.title: Automated macOS Installer
projects.description: Automated the process of creating a bootable USB for macOS/non-Apple devices on Linux & macOS environments.

projects.title: Pkg2Dmg
projects.description: Converted Apple's proprietary .pkg to .dmg formats using modified Pbzx for decompressing Apple archive chunks.

projects.title: Mobile Forensics [Android, iOS]
projects.description: In-depth analysis including Root, OEM Unlock (Bootloader), Custom ROM, De-Odex, iOS-exploit (checkm8, palra1n, etc.), DualBoot-IOS, Partition Management for Android & iOS.

projects.title: C2EFI - UEFI Forensics & Testing Utility
projects.description: Converts C source to UEFI .efi binary using GCC & LLD. Automates QEMU boot testing for UEFI binaries. Lightweight, Bash-based workflow for rapid .efi prototyping.

papers.title: ICS Auditor - Technical Compliance Framework
papers.description: An automated compliance auditing tool specifically designed for Industrial Control Systems (ICS). It evaluates infrastructure reports against cybersecurity frameworks like NIST SP 800-82 and ISA/IEC 62443, identifying configuration gaps and vulnerabilities in PLCs and HMIs. This research focuses on the automation of security posture validation in critical infrastructure.

papers.title: ESP32-S3 Based Reconnaissance & Attack Tool
papers.description: Completed research on using the ESP32-S3 microcontroller for offensive reconnaissance. Investigated whether low-cost devices can effectively attack systems and gather infrastructure intelligence within local networks via Wi-Fi and Bluetooth. Developed custom firmware to execute attacks like de-auth, packet flooding, and Bluetooth flooding. Validated the potential of cheap hardware in compromising infrastructure security.

papers.title: UEFI Disk Cloning Utility™ & Memory Operations
papers.description: This Paper discuss about my UEFI-based utility for forensic and security analysis, enabling full system memory dumping (including restricted regions) and sector-by-sector disk cloning. This tool provides critical low-level access for vulnerability detection and incident response before the operating system loads, and has been rigorously tested across virtualized and physical environments.

papers.title: Automated macOS Installer & Penetration Testing
papers.description: This project automates the creation of Hackintosh macOS installers and PKG-to-DMG converters within a Linux environment. It significantly streamlines complex setup processes like USB mapping and EFI configuration, achieving a 60% reduction in setup time and manual effort. The paper also includes vulnerability analysis using penetration of macOS and demonstrates few CVE's published.Moreover, it is also tested on various Versions of macOS to confirm whether the Vulenrability has been patched or not.it has mostly discussed about Privellege Escalation & RCE vulenrabilities via system or 3rd party app.

courses.title: Semester I
courses: Fundamentals in Computer Programming with C
